page.title: Hedging and Cautious Language
page.subtitle_html: Hedging and Cautious Language - C1 cautious claims, probability, limited evidence, and academic register.
page.subtitle_text: C1 cautious claims, probability, limited evidence, and academic register.

blocks.body: {"level":"C1","intro":"Hedging and Cautious Language: C1 anchors for making claims precise, cautious, and academically appropriate.","rules":[{"label":"Cautious verbs","text":"Use seem, appear, suggest, indicate, or tend to when evidence is not absolute.","example":"The findings seem to support this view."},{"label":"Modals of possibility","text":"Use may, might, could, and would appear to avoid overstatement.","example":"This may be related to the wider context."},{"label":"Limits and concessions","text":"Acknowledge limits with to some extent, not necessarily, or it should be acknowledged that.","example":"It should be acknowledged that the evidence remains limited."}]}

blocks.heading: C1 focus
blocks.body: This compact page exists as the theory anchor for the Polyglot English C1 hedging and cautious language lesson package.
